title: Testing, Static Quality, Performance, and Release · Retrieval Circuit
summary: Harden the service-data client with behavioral tests, deterministic fixtures, performance evidence, and a clean release. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a tested and release-ready JavaScript application after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design tests around observable behavior, changed inputs, boundaries, and failures rather than implementation lines.
Use fakes, stubs, spies, and controlled clocks only at owned boundaries without mocking away the behavior under test.
Await asynchronous outcomes, control timers and network fixtures, and fail on unhandled work deterministically.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.javascript:
// javascript-basics cumulative stakeholder program.
// Add one runnable behavior, assertion, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Test Behavior evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Test Behavior assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: A release candidate passes the familiar 3-record fixture but fails an empty list and a duplicate identifier. Reproduce both, add behavior and accessibility regressions, and reject release until the clean build agrees.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Test Behavior has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Test Behavior precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A release candidate passes the familiar 3-record fixture but fails an empty list and a duplicate identifier. Reproduce both, add behavior and accessibility regressions, and reject release until the clean build agrees.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: High line coverage proves the important behavior is correct.

options.text: Declare inputs, observable output, ownership, and the runtime behavior being tested
Trace values, bindings, receivers, mutations, and queued work before editing code
Implement the smallest behavior and retain an assertion over the actual result
Run boundary and changed-event cases, inspect failures, and remove accidental coupling

stimulus.title: Release Evidence case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A release candidate passes the familiar 5-record fixture but fails an empty list and a duplicate identifier. Reproduce both, add behavior and accessibility regressions, and reject release until the clean build agrees.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A successful local run is sufficient release proof.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Produce a clean-environment build, supported-runtime record, accessibility checks, rollback plan, and stakeholder acceptance evidence. Artifact under review: a tested and release-ready JavaScript application.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Release Evidence, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write the review evidence note for Release Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A release candidate passes the familiar 6-record fixture but fails an empty list and a duplicate identifier. Reproduce both, add behavior and accessibility regressions, and reject release until the clean build agrees.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Produce a clean-environment build, supported-runtime record, accessibility checks, rollback plan, and stakeholder acceptance evidence.

hints: Name the Release Evidence precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: A successful local run is sufficient release proof.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Test Behavior to the changed stakeholder case.
